key_points: Off-campus landlords remove advertised amenities after lease signing
Level27 cut shuttle service without notifying residents
Possums reportedly living in walls at Emerson Hall
key_quotes: They have been horrible to communicate with, as they frequently remove amenities without notice
how theres possums living in the fucking walls at emerson
The shuttle rarely operated. It got to the point that I didn't even try to use it

key_points: Party culture is intense and dominant — non-drinkers feel excluded
Greek life and uptown bars are central to social identity
Non-partiers do find community eventually but struggle early on
key_quotes: I'm some what a country lower income guy and I'm not a drinker or partier. So I've kinda scared myself that I'm not gonna make any friends
Brick Street can get out of control fast
drink a trashcan or mindprobe for me, if those still exist. I still remember being tanked at the bars by 7am

key_points: Western dining hall food quality declines sharply during semester
Uptown restaurant scene is a genuine saving grace
Bell Tower dining praised as best on-campus option
key_quotes: Western is a trick, nowadays during the actual semester the food is so bad, but there's SO many restaurants uptown
Bell Tower is the best
Mac n Joes praised by visitors as a local staple

key_points: Two on-campus food banks exist — sign of real financial stress
Non-traditional and minority students report feeling isolated and out of place
Disability accommodations exist but not prominently discussed by students
key_quotes: I'm a 29 year old non-traditional student having very intense second thoughts
When I was there the prejudice and racism from students was crazy. Being told I didn't belong here, in class
I was a black man, and while getting lunch, two white men walked past us and coughed a slur directed at me

key_points: Merit scholarships are generous and transparent — stats directly predict awards
International students get very limited need-based aid as a public school
Some students rejected outright for lacking financial resources to attend
key_quotes: they REJECTED me in January because 'it appears that you do not have the financial resources to attend Miami University'
Miami University (Ohio) publishes merit scholarship amounts based on your stats
I got into Miami University for Geography! I have no clue how I'll pay for it

key_points: Farmer School of Business is the crown jewel — strong undergrad teaching focus
Not getting direct Farmer admission is common; transfer path requires 3.5 GPA
Academic program cuts happening quietly while athletics get massive investment
key_quotes: The Farmer Faculty truly care about the students and put undergraduate teaching above all else
They are cutting academic programs left and right, quietly laying off non-tenured faculty
it used to be one of the best schools in Ohio, back in our parents' days Miami was where the smart people went

key_points: Board approved $281M arena despite clear student opposition
McGuffey Hall entrance collapsed — deferred maintenance is a real problem
Esports program killed without warning despite being used as recruiting pitch
key_quotes: Craw and the whole BOT can eat a bag of dicks. This is the absolute worst decision i have seen the university make
Jesus that could have easily killed someone
students came to Miami due to scholarships related to esports, now what happens to them?

key_points: Campus is genuinely stunning — brick Georgian architecture universally praised
Oxford is isolated rural Ohio; cold, gray winters are a real deterrent
Town is small but uptown bar/restaurant scene serves as social hub
key_quotes: don't fall for the summer views this tour shows. when you're in the middle of nowhere ohio for 40 degree temps, you will wonder why you didn't attend college somewhere farther south
The best decision of my life was transferring from Miami Ohio to college in Florida after my first semester
To quote Robert Frost: 'The most beautiful campus that ever there was'

key_points: Farmer IB club places nearly 100% of members into investment banking annually
Strong Midwest placement (Chicago, Cincinnati) but national reach requires extra effort
Cradle of Coaches legacy — NFL coaches, finance executives are real alumni outcomes
key_quotes: Investment banking club usually places 98-100% for each of its classes into IB ranging from BB, EB, and MM firms
My son just graduated from Farmers with a Finance degree and now works in investment banking in downtown Chicago
the top ~10% of people in my finance class ended up in New York with another 10-20% in SF

key_points: Merit scholarships make Miami genuinely affordable for high-stat students
Out-of-state and international sticker price is steep with little need-based help
University spending $281M on arena while cutting academic programs raises red flags
key_quotes: I think you can get the most out of it as long as you do the honors program. Also, they have great merit scholarships, so it's relatively affordable
Miami's debt will now be a few million dollars away from its budget. The most reckless financial decision I've ever heard of
For an SAT score of 1480+ among other requirements, Miami gives merit aid to internationals

key_points: Preppy, wealthy, white — students from rural or lower-income backgrounds feel it immediately
Campus beauty and school spirit are genuinely strong selling points
Fun and social but the party culture overshadows everything else for many students
key_quotes: I go to Miami University and I knew it was 'preppy' but I didn't think that actually meant anything. I find that I really can't stand most of the people around me
some people here are snooty stuck up and I'm somewhat a country lower income guy
Lol you'll be getting more lit than the people at Stanford and shit, I've heard from my friends Miami is extremely fun

key_points: **Racism:** Racial slurs shouted at Black students on campus and during admitted student events
**Infrastructure:** Building entrance collapsed from deferred maintenance; possums in dorm walls
**Administration:** $281M arena approved over student objection while academic programs are cut
key_quotes: I was a Black man at Make It Miami and two white men coughed a slur directly at me — this has forced me to rethink my decision
These guys have been doing this [shouting slurs from trucks] for over a decade
They're cutting academics, but spending millions on sports

key_points: Farmer School IB club is a near-guaranteed pipeline to top finance firms
Two discreet on-campus food banks available to any student, no questions asked
Basketball team's historic 2024-25 run created rare, electric campus energy
key_quotes: Investment banking club usually places 98-100% for each of its classes into IB
The one located in Phillips Hall is awesome, and the nutrition students sometimes hold events during pickup
Never thought I'd see Millet packed with a lively student section on national TV
